LinkedHashSet<Integer>.reasoning: In Python, the closest equivalent to Java's LinkedHashSet is an OrderedDict. An OrderedDict in Python maintains a doubly-linked list running through all of its entries, which defines the encounter order (iteration order), similar to how LinkedHashSet maintains insertion order in Java. The difference between these two data structures lies in their use cases and functionality. While an OrderedDict is more general purpose and can be used as a dictionary or set, LinkedHashSet is specifically designed for maintaining insertion order and offering additional methods like addFirst() and addLast().
